mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad economic strength and robust corporate hiring, leading to higher aggregate demand, improved earnings expectations, and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market supports consumer spending
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient of the signal's 3-month change versus 60-day forward returns is less than 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month period signals a cyclical downturn and potential recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns as investors anticipate falling corporate profits.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating (high YoY CPI change) while the Fed Funds rate is already elevated, it signals the central bank is behind the curve, forcing more aggressive future tightening that will harm corporate profits and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative average return (t-test p > 0.05) in triggered periods over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Following a recession, accelerating quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms a robust economic recovery, boosting investor confidence and driving equity prices higher as corporate earnings rebound from a low base.
failure_condition: The signal's rank IC is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) during periods defined as the first 8 quarters after a NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative month-over-month surprise in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors, leading to lower near-term earnings forecasts and negative short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a component of the Coincident Economic Index
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero at a 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected retail sales growth reflects robust consumer health, which directly supports revenues for a large portion of the S&P 500 and drives positive equity market sentiment over very short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (correctly predicting positive 5-day returns) is below the baseline positive day rate of 65.5% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a high absolute threshold, monetary policy is restrictive, increasing the cost of capital for firms and raising the discount rate for equity valuations, creating a headwind for forward returns.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward returns are positive when FEDFUNDS is above 5% during any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in unemployment and a decline in housing starts signals a sharp contraction in two critical, interest-rate sensitive sectors of the economy, foreshadowing a broader recession and causing a significant equity market drawdown.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 2008 GFC
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on trigger events fails to generate a positive information ratio over the 2005-2024 backtest period.
